response_body.response: need to know your skin type first. is it oily, dry, combination, or sensitive. that determines which actives and how aggressive you go. once you tell me that, i'll map out the exact routine and products.
if you're actually oily, the routine shifts. but i need clarity: are you oily all over, or just t-zone. and is your skin texture issue acne, scarring, roughness, or uneven tone.

you've got oily skin, so you want something lightweight that won't clog you. look for a moisturizer with **ceramides + panthenol**. ceramides lock in hydration and repair your barrier without the heaviness, and panthenol soothes and reduces redness. apply it after your active (azelaic or niacinamide in the AM, or centella in the PM) on slightly damp skin so it absorbs better. specific brand recs aren't in your current docs. what's your budget, and are you dealing with any irritation or sensitivity right now.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
products.description: 10% niacinamide for sebum control and pore appearance.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.
